question: Can JAX-emitted StableHLO bytecode (.mlirbc) slot into the rvLLM tpu_custom_call body?
hypothesis: TpuMosaicSerializedBody validator only checks ML\xefR magic, so PJRT might accept any MLIR bytecode at the body slot.
error: PJRT_Client_Compile failed: Failed to deserialize the Mosaic module: INTERNAL: Missing or invalid version attribute
key_findings: Validator (m2_tpu_custom_call.rs:26) accepts the bytecode -- magic ML\xefR matches.
PJRT execution-time deserializer ENFORCES Mosaic dialect via the stable_mosaic.version attr that JAX-emitted StableHLO does not produce.

JAX dead-code-eliminates unused operands during lowering -- the probe's identity function returned only (hidden, kv_in), losing positions/layer_offsets/expert_directory/w1_block_t/w1_row_scales from the operand ABI.
implication: The body slot strictly requires Mosaic-typed memref bodies with version attrs. JAX-extract-as-bytecode is not a valid shortcut to a real-MLP body.

remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.name: pure_stablehlo_inline_layer
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.approach: Emit M2 layer math (RMSNorm + attention + MoE + MLP + residual) as inline StableHLO ops in m2_decode_graph.rs::emit_layer_call. Drop the tpu_custom_call entirely.
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.perf: 2-4x slower than Mosaic (per Agent 6) but correct PPL

remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.name: pallas_kernel_lower_to_mosaic
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.approach: Write M2 layer as pl.pallas_call kernel, lower via jax.jit, mosaic-serde produces valid Mosaic bytecode with version attrs.
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.ppl_quality: matches JAX 4.922 once Pallas kernel parity verified

remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.name: hand_lowered_mosaic_with_split_calls
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.approach: Split each layer into 3-4 smaller tpu_custom_call ops (RMS+attn / MoE+W1 / silu+W3+W2 / residual), each with hand-written lowered Mosaic body that fits the per-body size budget.
remaining_paths_to_real_ppl.ppl_quality: matches JAX 4.922 once body math verified
